2014-03-26.title: Pollution Killed 7 Million People Worldwide in 2012, Report Finds
2014-03-26.abstract: A World Health Organization report concluded that more than one-third of pollution deaths in 2012 occurred in fast-developing Asian nations.

2014-03-26.title: On Visit to China, Michelle Obama Eases In Some Political Messages
2014-03-26.abstract: What seemed to start as a spring break holiday for the first lady has turned out to be far more substantive than what the cheerful advocate of fitness and healthful eating often displays at home.

2014-03-26.title: Protesting in Venezuela, With Antipathy Toward Cuba’s Government
2014-03-26.abstract: Many Venezuelan protesters have also focused their resentment on Cuba’s government, which, they say, has leeched off Venezuela’s oil wealth and grafted its rigid brand of socialism onto their country.

2014-03-26.title: U.S. Challenge Now Is to Stop Further Putin Moves
2014-03-26.abstract: Officials in the United States and Europe have privately concluded that Crimea is lost and that the real challenge is stopping Russia from further destabilizing Ukraine.

2014-03-26.title: Qaeda Militants Seek Syria Base, U.S. Officials Say
2014-03-26.abstract: Intelligence assessments say that Al Qaeda’s leadership is developing a long-term plan to create cells in Syria to train Westerners to carry out plots against targets in the West.

2014-03-27.title: Amnesty International Study Finds Executions Rose in 2013
2014-03-27.abstract: Amnesty International’s report attributed most of the nearly 15 percent increase to “virtual killing sprees” carried out by Iran and Iraq, though the group considers China the world’s top executioner.

2014-03-27.title: Guinea: Government Bans Bat Soup to Halt Ebola Outbreak
2014-03-27.abstract: The action came after the West African nation’s first Ebola outbreak, which has killed 63 people.

2014-03-27.title: Indonesia Candidate Tied to Human Rights Abuses Stirs Unease
2014-03-27.abstract: Prabowo Subianto, an ex-special forces commander accused of torture and abductions, has announced his candidacy for president, putting the Obama administration in a difficult position.

2014-03-27.title: Military Cuts Render NATO Less Formidable as Deterrent to Russia
2014-03-27.abstract: President Obama and European leaders have pledged that Russia would not be allowed to run roughshod, but the military reality tells a different story.

2014-03-27.title: German Man to Return Nazi-Looted Art
2014-03-27.abstract: Cornelius Gurlitt, the octogenarian hoarder of art plundered by the Nazis, will return the paintings to their original Jewish owners or those owners’ descendants.

2014-03-28.title: Iraq: Bombs Kill at Least 33 in Baghdad
2014-03-28.abstract: A series of bombings in Baghdad killed at least 33 people and wounded dozens more on Thursday, Iraqi officials said.

2014-03-28.title: Human Rights Panel Criticizes U.S.
2014-03-28.abstract: A United Nations human rights panel has called for more oversight and transparency in the program of targeted drone strikes carried out by the United States.

2014-03-28.title: U.S. Investigates Brothers From Ecuador With Ties to Campaigns
2014-03-28.abstract: The fugitives are being investigated to determine whether any of the millions the men were accused of pilfering in their home country made it to the United States.

2014-03-28.title: Pakistani Gets Death Penalty for Blasphemy
2014-03-28.abstract: A case that set off rioting last year in a Christian neighborhood is now likely to put a spotlight on capital punishment in Pakistan, which has never carried out an execution under its blasphemy laws.

2014-03-28.title: Obama Seeks to Calm Saudis as Paths Split
2014-03-28.abstract: Saudi Arabia’s leaders are feeling increasingly compelled to go their own way in dealing with Iran, Syria, Egypt and the Muslim Brotherhood.

2014-03-29.title: First Cases Documented of TB Caught From Cats
2014-03-29.abstract: The four human cases were related to a rare cluster of sick cats in southern England, but TB experts reassured pet owners that they had virtually nothing to fear.

2014-03-29.title: U.N. Official Denounces Syria on Aid Access
2014-03-29.abstract: Valerie Amos, the head of emergency relief, pointed to the Syrian government’s denials of access and continued use of barrel bombs — both blatant violations of international law.

2014-03-29.title: In Hunt for Lost Jet, New Sightings of Debris Are Promising but Inconclusive
2014-03-29.abstract: Officials involved in the search, mindful of the amount of detritus adrift in the world’s oceans, cautioned that the sightings are inconclusive so far.

2014-03-29.title: Obama Ends Overseas Trip With Award for Saudi
2014-03-29.abstract: The brief private ceremony came a day after the president chose not to raise the issue of human rights during a two-hour discussion with King Abdullah.

2014-03-29.title: Taliban Attack Election Panel Headquarters Before Afghan Voting
2014-03-29.abstract: The attack by the Taliban, who have vowed to violently derail the coming election for a new president, was the third in Kabul just this week.

2014-03-30.title: Credibility of Afghan Vote in Doubt as Observers Flee Violence
2014-03-30.abstract: Many international election monitors curtailed their activities or made plans to evacuate their foreign employees, potentially raising serious questions about the election’s validity.

2014-03-30.title: Leo Bretholz, 93, Dies; Escaped Train to Auschwitz
2014-03-30.abstract: Mr. Bretholz testified in Congress in 2011, asking that Holocaust survivors be allowed to sue the French railway S.N.C.F. for its role in Nazi deportations to death camps.

2014-03-30.title: More Ships and Planes Join Search for Jetliner
2014-03-30.abstract: Ten planes and ten ships scoured the latest search area, roughly the size of Poland, and others were scheduled to arrive in the next several days.

2014-03-30.title: In Our Pages: March 31
2014-03-30.abstract: Highlights from the International Herald Tribune archives: A steamer cut in two after a collision at sea in 1889, and an associate of the heavyweight boxer Sonny Liston denies fronting for criminals in 1964.

2014-03-30.title: Large Crowds Fill Taipei Streets in Protest Over China Trade Bill
2014-03-30.abstract: A pact that would drop barriers to trade in services with China has spurred concern in Taiwan, where student-led demonstrators continue to occupy the legislature.

2014-03-31.title: Panel’s Warning on Climate Risk: Worst Is Yet to Come
2014-03-31.abstract: A United Nations report warned that climate change is already having sweeping effects on every continent and is likely to grow substantially worse unless greenhouse emissions are brought under control.

2014-03-31.title: Morocco’s ‘Mule Ladies’
2014-03-31.abstract: Moroccan women face a dangerous daily toil, carrying large bales of duty-free goods back from the Spanish North African enclave of Melilla.

2014-03-31.title: Journalists Held by Militants in Syria Reunite With Family
2014-03-31.abstract: Javier Espinosa and Ricardo García Vilanova arrived in Madrid a day after Mr. Espinosa called his newspaper to say they had been handed over to Turkish soldiers.

2014-03-31.title: Not on Ballot, Turkey’s Premier Still Gets Push From Party in Local Elections
2014-03-31.abstract: Voters delivered what appeared to be a resounding victory for Prime Minister Recep Tayyip Erdogan’s Islamist-rooted party in local elections that took on national importance as a referendum on Mr. Erdogan’s 11 years in power.

2014-03-31.title: A Borderline Where Women Bear the Weight
2014-03-31.abstract: Morocco’s “mule ladies” haul goods into the country from Spain, earning as little as three euros per trip. As hard times push men toward these jobs, the working conditions have become dangerous.
